description: LightRAG v1.4.x + RAG-Anything analysis for CSV structural blindness
experiment_status: code_analysis_only (API quota exhausted for runtime test)

lightrag_v14_csv_handling.ainsert_interface: Same as v1.3.8: accepts plain text string, no CSV-specific parsing
lightrag_v14_csv_handling.chunking: Token-based chunking on plain text, no column/row awareness
lightrag_v14_csv_handling.extraction_prompt: Default entity extraction prompt, no table structure directives
lightrag_v14_csv_handling.conclusion: LightRAG v1.4.12 ainsert() for standalone CSV input is functionally equivalent to v1.3.8 naive serialization

rag_anything.purpose: Multi-modal document parsing pipeline using MinerU
rag_anything.table_handling: TableModalProcessor targets tables embedded in PDFs/DOCX (parsed by MinerU), not standalone CSV files
rag_anything.csv_path: For standalone CSV, RAG-Anything falls through to LightRAG.ainsert() with plain text - no topology recognition, no schema induction, no constrained extraction
rag_anything.dependency: Requires mineru[core] (unavailable on Python 3.14, installed via Python 3.11 venv)
rag_anything.conclusion: RAG-Anything does not address the structural blindness problem for statistical CSV ingestion

findings.paper_implication: Existing Baseline results (LightRAG v1.3.8 naive serialization) are representative of the current official pipeline's behavior on CSV input. The structural blindness problem persists in v1.4.x because the core ainsert() path has not changed for text input.

code_evidence.lightrag_ainsert_signature: async def ainsert(self, input: str | list[str], ...)
code_evidence.no_csv_parser_in_lightrag: grep for 'csv|CSV|tabular' in lightrag source shows only export functions, no CSV input parsing
code_evidence.raganything_table_processor: TableModalProcessor imported from lightrag.modalprocessors, designed for MinerU-parsed table elements from PDFs

script_created: evaluation/lightrag_latest_baseline.py (ready to run when API quota available)
